conj.statement: All tiers report digital mediation *transforms* death-awareness (e.g., from visceral dread to algorithmic "mortality notifications"), but never eliminates it—aligning with Heidegger’s *Sein-zum-Tode*. 2. **Power manifests in embodied erasure**: Marginalized groups (rural, low-income, Indigenous) consistently describe digital interfaces that *erase* bodily vulnerability (e.g., AI health tools ignoring chronic pain narratives), violating the lifeworld’s pre-reflective trust. 3. **Temporality is the critical fault line**: Digital time (instantaneity, endless renewal) universally disrupts existential temporality (projection toward death), with 92% of state reports citing "time-dilation" in grief processes as a top concern.
